title: Developer Experience (DX)
summary: Fast feedback loops, local development environments, toolchain standardization, and measuring developer productivity.

sections.content:
## What is Developer Experience?

Developer Experience (DX) is the sum of all interactions a developer has with systems, tools, and processes while building software. Good DX means:
- Fast feedback loops (local test run in seconds, not minutes)
- Low friction to start contributing
- Clear, discoverable tooling
- Consistent environments (no 'works on my machine')
- Self-service — no waiting for other teams

sections.title: DX Feedback Loop
sections.content:
graph LR
    Code[Write code] -->|fast build| Test[Run tests]
    Test -->|instant feedback| Fix[Fix issues]
    Fix --> Code
    Slow[Slow loop\n20 min build] -->|context switch| Frustrated[Frustrated dev]
    Fast[Fast loop\n30 sec] -->|flow state| Productive[Productive dev]
    style Fast fill:#059669,color:#fff
    style Slow fill:#dc2626,color:#fff
    style Productive fill:#059669,color:#fff

## Local Development Environment

### Docker Compose for local dev
```yaml
# docker-compose.dev.yml
services:
  order-service:
    build:
      context: .
      target: dev        # use dev stage with hot-reload
    volumes:
      - ./src:/app/src   # mount source for live reload
    environment:
      SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE: dev
      DATABASE_URL: jdbc:postgresql://postgres:5432/orders
    depends_on:
      - postgres
      - kafka

  postgres:
    image: postgres:15-alpine
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: orders
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: dev
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data

  kafka:
    image: confluentinc/cp-kafka:7.5.0
    environment:
      KAFKA_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS: PLAINTEXT://kafka:9092

  # One command to spin up everything:
  # docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up
```

### Tilt — Live update K8s in dev
```python
# Tiltfile
docker_build('order-service', '.', 
  live_update=[
    sync('./src', '/app/src'),   # sync code changes instantly
    run('mvn compile -q'),       # compile without full rebuild
  ]
)
k8s_yaml('k8s/dev/')
k8s_resource('order-service', port_forwards='8080:8080')
```

## Measuring Developer Productivity

### DORA Metrics (team-level)
- Deployment frequency
- Lead time for changes
- Change failure rate
- Mean time to restore (MTTR)

### SPACE Framework (individual)
- **S**atisfaction — developer happiness/engagement
- **P**erformance — outcome quality
- **A**ctivity — actions and outputs
- **C**ommunication — collaboration quality
- **E**fficiency — flow state, context switching

### Developer Survey Questions
```
1. How long does it take to set up a new dev environment? (< 1hr / 1-4hrs / > 4hrs)
2. How fast is the local test suite? (< 1min / 1-5min / > 5min)
3. How often do you wait for CI? (rarely / sometimes / always blocks me)
4. How easy is it to deploy to staging? (1 click / few steps / need help)
5. How often do you context-switch due to slow tools? (never / sometimes / constantly)
```

rows.Better Practice: Incremental builds, Maven daemon, build cache

rows.Anti-pattern: Different JDK versions on each dev machine
rows.Better Practice: Dev Containers or SDKMAN version pinning

rows.Anti-pattern: File a ticket to get dev DB access
Manual environment config on each machine

sections.question: What is the primary benefit of using Dev Containers for a Java development team?
options: Dev Containers make Java applications run faster
All developers get an identical, pre-configured environment — eliminating 'works on my machine' issues and onboarding friction
Dev Containers allow Java code to run on any cloud platform
They automatically fix security vulnerabilities in dependencies
sections.explanation: Dev Containers define the entire development environment as code (JDK version, tools, VS Code extensions, ports). Every developer and CI uses the same container image. This eliminates version mismatch bugs, reduces onboarding time from days to minutes, and ensures consistency between local development and CI.
